1.Title: RPS Drop with Stable Users
1.Insight: For the endpoint Homepage, despite a constant load of **10 virtual users** for most of the test, the [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped sharply to **1.9 RPS** at the end, indicating a possible bottleneck in the server or network.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) stayed consistent at 10 from 11:14 AM UTC to 11:16 AM UTC, dropping to 7 at 11:16 AM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated between 4.8 and 6.0 RPS, peaking at **6.0 RPS** at 11:16 AM UTC, but sank to **1.9 RPS** at 11:16 AM UTC despite only a minor reduction in users.
1.Recommendation: Consider investigating server resource constraints, connection limits, or external factors (e.g. firewall, network saturation) that could reduce throughput, especially near the end of the load test.

2.Title: Response Time Fluctuations
2.Insight: Endpoint Homepage showed **highly variable response times** under steady user load, suggesting backend instability or intermittent performance degradation.
2.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped sharply from 3630ms at 11:14 AM UTC to 1930ms at 11:14 AM UTC, then climbed to a peak of **2590ms** at 11:15 AM UTC. Values fluctuated between 1810ms and 2340ms from 11:15 AM UTC to 11:16 AM UTC, indicating unpredictable server performance.
2.Recommendation: Consider profiling server-side application performance during similar load patterns, focusing on infrastructure bottlenecks such as CPU, memory, or queuing. Application-level monitoring may help pinpoint the cause of response variability.

3.Title: Late Test Instability
3.Insight: A combined drop in both [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) for Homepage at the end of the run could indicate either load engine resource exhaustion or active throttling on the server.
3.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) decreased from 10 to 7 at 11:16 AM UTC. At the same time, [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) fell to 1.9 RPS, implying reduced throughput even though user count decreased only marginally.
3.Recommendation: If engine resource metrics are available, check for high [Engine CPU](#EngineHealthMetrics) or [Engine Memory](#EngineHealthMetrics) usage. If not, consider server logs for evidence of throttling or overload protection activating in the final phase.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Client-side response times fluctuated, and throughput dropped in later stages, indicating backend constraints.
